name: Verona — Per-Region Pluggable Strategies and Behaviour-Oriented Concurrency
summary: Microsoft Research / Imperial / Uppsala's Verona is a concurrent OO language where every object lives in exactly one of a forest of isolated regions, and a thread has at most one 'window of mutability' — the active region — at a time. Per-region pluggable memory strategies (trace, RC, arena) are chosen by the programmer; reference capabilities (iso/mut/imm) enforce isolation. Behaviour-Oriented Concurrency (OOPSLA 2023) and Dynamic Region Ownership (PLDI 2025) extend the model.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Microsoft Research / Imperial / Uppsala's **Verona** is a concurrent OO language where every object lives in exactly one of a *forest of isolated regions*, and a thread has at most one "window of mutability" — the active region — at a time. Verona is the most ambitious modern composition of regions + concurrency + memory-strategy choice.

Status (as of 2026-04): research project at Microsoft, with active publications and an open-source repository. Not aimed at production use yet; the design is the contribution.

sections.title: Per-region pluggable strategies and reference capabilities
sections.content:
The distinctive contribution: **per-region pluggable memory strategies** (trace, RC, arena) chosen by the programmer, so a region's policy doesn't bleed into the rest of the program (Cheeseman et al., OOPSLA 2023). One region can be a tracing-GC region, a sibling region can be reference-counted, and a third can be an arena — each suited to its data shape and lifetime — without requiring a single global memory strategy.

**Reference-capability types** — `iso`, `mut`, `imm`, plus capabilities for moving objects between regions/threads — enforce isolation with no atomic ops on data accesses. `iso` is an isolation capability that names the region root; `mut` is mutable access within the active region; `imm` is shared immutable access. Capabilities and the active-region invariant together guarantee data-race freedom statically.

sections.title: Behaviour-Oriented Concurrency (OOPSLA 2023)
sections.content:
**Behaviour-Oriented Concurrency** (BoC, OOPSLA 2023) replaces actors with `when` behaviours that asynchronously acquire exclusive access to multiple `cown`-wrapped regions atomically — solving the "atomic update of N actors" problem actors leave open. A `when (a, b, c) { ... }` block is scheduled to run when exclusive access to `a`, `b`, and `c` can be granted simultaneously, eliminating the deadlock and re-entrancy problems that arise when independent actors must coordinate across multiple state updates.

BoC sits between actors and STM in the design space: stronger atomicity than actors, no rollback like STM, and statically scheduled rather than optimistic.

sections.title: Dynamic Region Ownership (PLDI 2025)
sections.content:
**Dynamic Region Ownership** (PLDI 2025) is a *dynamic* enforcement of the same discipline retrofitted into Python, relevant to the free-threaded CPython work. The static Verona design assumes a cooperative type system; for a dynamic language like Python, the same isolation invariants are enforced at runtime through capability tracking on objects.

This matters because as CPython removes the GIL, application code needs a coordination model that does not depend on a global mutex; Dynamic Region Ownership gives Python a region-based isolation model without requiring Python source-level type annotations.
